title: Terraform Resources and Data Sources
learningObjectives: Write resource blocks with required and optional arguments
Use data sources to query existing infrastructure
Use count and for_each for resource iteration

theory.content:
Resource Blocks

Resources are the most important element in Terraform. Each resource block describes one or more infrastructure objects (EC2 instances, S3 buckets, DNS records, etc.).

resource "aws_s3_bucket" "app_data" {
  bucket = "myapp-data-${var.environment}"

  tags = local.common_tags
}

resource "aws_s3_bucket_versioning" "app_data" {
  bucket = aws_s3_bucket.app_data.id
  versioning_configuration {
    status = "Enabled"
  }
}

Data Sources

Data sources allow Terraform to fetch information from existing infrastructure or external sources, without managing it.

# Fetch the latest Amazon Linux 2 AMI
data "aws_ami" "amazon_linux" {
  most_recent = true
  owners      = ["amazon"]
  filter {
    name   = "name"
    values = ["amzn2-ami-hvm-*-x86_64-gp2"]
  }
}

resource "aws_instance" "web" {
  ami = data.aws_ami.amazon_linux.id
}

Resource Dependencies

Implicit: Reference another resource's attribute — Terraform infers the dependency.

Explicit: Use depends_on for non-attribute dependencies.

resource "aws_instance" "app" {
  # Implicit: waits for SG to be created
  vpc_security_group_ids = [aws_security_group.app.id]
  
  # Explicit: waits for IAM role policies
  depends_on = [aws_iam_role_policy.app_policy]
}

count and for_each

# count
resource "aws_instance" "web" {
  count         = var.instance_count
  ami           = data.aws_ami.amazon_linux.id
  instance_type = "t3.micro"
  tags = { Name = "web-${count.index}" }
}

# for_each with a map
resource "aws_s3_bucket" "data" {
  for_each = toset(["logs", "backups", "artifacts"])
  bucket   = "myapp-${each.key}-${var.environment}"
}

Resources Documentation →
